ground_truth_fix: There are two issues here. First is an off by 1 error in the loop, you need to account for the width of the read in the for loop condition: That is because the verifier has to check for every possible iteration if the body of the loop is valid. But since data_end is a u32 without limits that becomes to much. What we can do to fix that is to set an upper limit for the number of iterations. like: This should be functionally the same. In this case I chose 1500 as the max iteration count since most packets will never reach that size. Though you might need to tune that.

candidate_repairs.patch_hint: Add an explicit unsigned clamp and keep the offset calculation in a separate verified register
candidate_repairs.rationale: A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.
